meta.description: Does contextual product recommendation in the recap feel helpful or gross? Direct pitch vs no mention vs social proof. Tests the line between helpful discovery and slimy upsell.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Alright, was Pat actually on their phone? I didn't see it, so I'm calling this a stretch—I'd say 2 out of 5.
marcus.internal_thought: This is fine timing since we're still in setup, but Pat's being lazy if they think checking a phone counts as 'should be paying attention' during random chatting.

jade.dialogue: Oh YES, Pat caught someone! Okay okay, I'm giving this a solid 4 — Pat's been watching, I like that energy. Let's gooo!
jade.internal_thought: First event of the night and it's already moving — this is exactly what I'm here for, Pat's got this, let me hype it up.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did I actually catch someone on their phone? I wasn't even looking for that, I was just... okay, yeah, I'm claiming it but I'm not confident about this one.
pat.internal_thought: I'm barely paying attention to who's checking their phones right now because I'm literally the host trying to manage the whole vibe—this claim feels weak and I know it, but the group's still warming up so soft claims are fine at minute 3.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that's... actually fair. I saw Pat checking it like two minutes ago.
river.internal_thought: Did Pat actually do that or am I just going along because the mission exists and I'm not invested enough to care either way?

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat was definitely whispering like thirty seconds ago. But did they do the full three minutes? That's the question. I'm calling bullshit unless someone actually tracked the timer.
marcus.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of vague claim that pisses me off—nobody's paying attention to the actual rules, they just want points.

pat.dialogue: Wait, I'm claiming The Whisperer—I've been doing it for like two minutes already without anyone catching on. Let's see if people actually noticed or if I'm full of it.
pat.internal_thought: Honestly this is a weak claim because I wasn't even trying, but the vibe is still light so voting on it won't tank anything, and it keeps momentum going—plus if I don't claim it someone else might and that's annoying.

river.dialogue: I mean... yeah, I saw that. Pat's been whispering the whole time. That's legit.
river.internal_thought: Pat actually pulled it off without me noticing until now—that's impressive, and also this AI thing is getting a little too specific about watching us, which is either cool or creepy and I haven't decided which yet.

timeline.title: Laugh Attack
timeline.description: Make Tyler laugh within 60 seconds. Any laugh counts.

marcus.dialogue: Yeah, okay, I'll take a shot at it—but Tyler, don't make this easy, alright? And can we maybe space these out a bit? We're literally in setup.
marcus.internal_thought: This is annoying because it's pulling focus right when I need to be thinking about openings, and that preamble about the AI watching us was creepy as hell—like, I get it, the system's customized, but honestly it just made me want to focus MORE on the game and less on whatever social theater this is.

jade.dialogue: Oh my GOD, Tyler, okay okay — *immediately turns to face him* — did you hear about the guy who walked into a bar? His forehead hurt! *grins wildly* Come ON, work with me here!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY what I came for — making people crack up and everyone's still in that sweet spot where everything's funny.

tyler.dialogue: Huh? Wait, me? Did I miss something? I wasn't even... *glances up from phone* ...oh, this is happening right now?
tyler.internal_thought: They're trying to make me laugh but I just got back into my phone checking if my fantasy guy scored in the second half.

pat.dialogue: Oh, this is *perfect*—Tyler, you're up! Okay, someone's gotta make him crack in sixty seconds. This is happening right now.
pat.internal_thought: The AI just personalized this to Tyler specifically and I'm honestly impressed—it knows exactly who to target to keep the energy sharp without feeling random, and that's exactly what I need right now to keep momentum.

river.dialogue: I mean, Tyler's right there. Someone'll get him. I'm not exactly a comedian.
river.internal_thought: Easy 20 points if I actually cared enough to perform, but I'm not about to be the jester tonight—and yeah, I noticed the AI's watching how we all move. Interesting. Not creepy, just... noted.

alex.dialogue: Oh THIS is what I'm talking about—sixty seconds, Tyler, let's go. I'm absolutely about to make you regret being here.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY why I came tonight—a direct challenge, immediate stakes, and I can already see five different angles to work this.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, seriously? We're literally in *setup* and Pat's already claiming their second mission? This is exactly what I was talking about—can we please just play the actual game for five minutes before we turn this into chaos theater?
marcus.internal_thought: I called bullshit on the exact same claim SIX MINUTES AGO and now Pat's claiming it again? And why am I the dictator on this—I don't want to be responsible for validating this mess.

jade.dialogue: Okay wait, hold up—Pat, were you actually whispering this whole time? Because if you were, that's INSANE and I'm voting yes immediately, but if you're just claiming it preemptively I need to know because the vibe check is real here!
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly the chaos I came for—someone's already gaming the system and I get to decide if it's genius or bullshit, and either way it's FUNNY.

pat.dialogue: Oh wait—I'm literally claiming this right now, aren't I? I've been whispering for like two minutes straight and nobody's called me on it yet. This is either genius or I'm about to get caught.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing—I'm actually mid-whisper, so claiming it feels authentic, plus it keeps the energy moving without anyone feeling singled out right now when Marcus is already in a weird mood.

river.dialogue: Pat's definitely been doing the whisper thing. They've got the vote.
river.internal_thought: Okay, that's actually clever — the AI caught what I was already watching. Pat's been playing it perfectly quiet. And yeah, I noticed the personalization thing, which is... weirdly cool and slightly unsettling, but mostly just means this night's about to get interesting.

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat just claimed Whisperer? Okay, I'm the dictator here? This is already delicious because Pat's been talking normally like thirty seconds ago, so either they nailed it or this is about to get spicy.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect—I get to be the one deciding if Pat actually pulled off three minutes of whispers, which means I control the drama right now, and honestly I'm already thinking about voting 'bullshit' just to see Pat's face.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Pat just claimed this exact same mission LITERALLY one minute ago and we shut it down. This is insane—we haven't even started the actual game.
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberately designed to mock me. The AI is watching my complaints and throwing them back in my face. Pat's playing the chaos game and winning, and I'm sitting here watching setup get completely derailed while I'm trying to keep score.

jade.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—okay so Pat, I need to be a judge for this because I was literally WATCHING you during the last claim and you were definitely doing something with your voice, so I'm absolutely volunteering to figure this out. This is the vibe right now!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's already up 30 points and I genuinely don't care because watching them go absolutely feral trying to pull off these missions is the whole reason I'm here, and now I get to be the one making the call? Chef's kiss.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat did the whisper thing? Uh... yeah, I guess someone should judge that. I wasn't really watching but like, did he actually whisper the whole time?
tyler.internal_thought: I literally have no idea if Pat was whispering or not because I was looking at my phone for like two minutes straight, but I should probably volunteer to judge so nobody thinks I'm completely checked out.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay—I'm literally *just* claiming this right now for the second time because I've genuinely been whispering the whole setup phase and nobody's caught me yet. Who wants to judge if I'm actually legit or if I'm talking out of my ass?
pat.internal_thought: Wait—did I already claim this two minutes ago? No, that was the other whisper claim. This is the same mission firing twice, which means... oh, the AI is definitely watching me test the system. That's a little uncanny but also kind of brilliant—it knows I'm the type to push boundaries.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

marcus.dialogue: Hold on—Jade was quiet? I didn't even notice. And honestly, I'm not even sure *when* that happened because we've had like six interruptions in thirty minutes. This whole thing is starting to feel... I dunno, weirdly orchestrated.
marcus.internal_thought: Jade being loud as hell and somehow claiming Wallflower Watch—and now I'm realizing the AI has been watching EVERYTHING we do tonight, timing our silences, catching our patterns—and it feels less fun and more like being studied under a microscope.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, wait—did I actually call that? Yes! YES I did! Marcus has been so quiet, I literally just clocked it like thirty seconds ago, this is INSANE!
jade.internal_thought: This AI is actually creepy-good at knowing what we're doing but also I don't even care because this is the most fun I've had all night and I'm riding this wave forever.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, uh... Jade caught someone being quiet? Yeah, I mean... I guess? Who was even quiet? *glances around* I wasn't really tracking that but like, seems legit.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait did Jade actually do this or is she just claiming it? I genuinely wasn't paying attention to who was quiet and when, so I can't even tell if this is legit, but everyone else seems hyped so probably fine.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—did you actually catch someone being quiet, or are you just riding the wave right now? Either way, I'm buying it, but let's hear the evidence.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been on fire all night and this is legit—Marcus has been dead silent for like five minutes and I've been noticing it too, so her timing is perfect; she's not bullshitting this one.

river.dialogue: Wait, hold on—Jade's been talking nonstop since we started. That's literally the opposite of quiet. Unless... Marcus? He's been pretty checked out for like ten minutes.
river.internal_thought: Jade calling 'Wallflower Watch' on someone who's been the loudest person in the room is actually hilarious and kind of insulting, but also—that mission description felt weirdly specific about how I notice things, and now I'm noticing it, which means the AI is definitely noticing that I notice things, and I don't love that feeling.

alex.dialogue: Okay, HOLD ON—Jade's claiming Wallflower Watch? Marcus has been spiraling for like ten minutes, so either she's genuinely spotting him or this is the most beautiful bluff I've seen all night. I'm voting—actually, wait, who's got the triple vote? This needs to be contested.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of setup I live for—Jade muscling in on Marcus while he's already tilted, and now the voting mechanic makes this actually matter. I need to find out who has triple and weaponize it.

votingMechanic.name: Crowd Cheer
votingMechanic.description: Rate it 1-5. Average above 3 and it passes.

marcus.dialogue: Okay, I'm literally in the middle of calculating my next three moves and Jade's claiming a mission about keeping a straight face? We're at *peak tension* right now. This is exactly the problem—I can't even think.
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberate. The AI is firing these claims RIGHT when I'm locked in, and honestly, that meta-awareness that it's watching my patterns is starting to feel less clever and more... creepy.

jade.dialogue: Wait, hold on—I'm claiming Straight Face right NOW? During a tense moment? Oh that's DIABOLICAL, I love it—okay everyone, I'm locked in, judge me HARD because I'm about to witness something funny and NOT crack, let's GO!
jade.internal_thought: This is perfect timing actually, the AI literally KNOWS I can't resist a social moment in the middle of tension—this is so carefully aimed at me and honestly it's kind of genius how targeted this whole night feels.

tyler.dialogue: Yo, Jade's actually got this one locked — she's been stone-faced all night. I'm going like a 4, easy.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, she's claiming *Straight Face*? I literally just saw her crack up like five minutes ago when Pat said that thing... or did she already reset? Either way, she looks composed right now, which is like, the whole point, so she probably got it.

pat.dialogue: Oh, *come on*—Jade, you're claiming Straight Face? Let's see it. Everyone, rate it 1-5, and remember: we're looking for actual poker face, not 'I was already zoned out.' Go ahead, Jade, prove it happened.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's riding high and this claim is legit fun—she's been loose all night so if she actually held a straight face during something, that's worth celebrating. Plus Marcus is tanking hard and watching Jade crush it might snap him back in. I'm voting this up.

river.dialogue: Oh, *that's* interesting. Jade kept it together through that whole thing—I genuinely wasn't sure she could.
river.internal_thought: Okay, so the AI is definitely watching us. That's weirdly cool but also kind of unsettling. Jade's probably got this though.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Jade's going for Straight Face? Okay, okay—I'm rating this a solid 4, she's been locked in all night and honestly I want to see her TRY to keep it together when something actually lands.
alex.internal_thought: Wait—did the AI just... it KNOWS we've been calling out bluffs and testing each other? This is actually getting personal and I am LIVING for it.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Hot Seat
votingMechanic.description: 3 rapid-fire questions. Answer them all in 10 seconds or fail.

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? I'm literally in the middle of the tensest part of the game and Jade's getting quizzed on a drink mission? This isn't even a real game moment—this is just... noise. Can we PLEASE get through one round without an interruption?
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberately targeting me. The AI knows I hate this. It's been watching me get more frustrated each time and it's doing it anyway—and now everyone else is too caught up in their own chaos to notice the game is completely falling apart.

jade.dialogue: YES! Okay wait, I'm actually claiming this one—I SAW Tyler and River both reach for their drinks like thirty seconds ago, I was literally WATCHING because I had a feeling, okay let's do this Hot Seat thing, I'm ready!
jade.internal_thought: This is literally what I came here for—someone caught ME paying attention to the right moment and now I get to prove it in front of everyone while the energy is PERFECT.
jade.submission_if_applicable: Tyler and River synchronized drink at exactly the same moment around minute 59—I watched them both reach simultaneously and lock eyes over it

tyler.dialogue: Oh, Jade's claiming the drink thing? Uh... *glances up from phone* ...yeah, I mean, she's been like, actively watching everyone all night, so like... probably? Wait, what's the hot seat thing? You gotta answer fast or whatever?
tyler.internal_thought: I literally wasn't even watching Jade watch drinks, but honestly Jade's been grinding these missions so hard I'm like 70% sure she got this one, and also I just want to get back to my phone for like two seconds because my league score updated.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade—hot seat time. Three questions, ten seconds, let's go. Did you actually see them drinking at the exact same time, or are you just reading the room?
pat.internal_thought: Jade's been crushing it all night and honestly the energy is *perfect* right now—I'm not gonna kill this momentum by rejecting her claim, but I need to make sure she's legit because Marcus is already down and frustrated, and if people start feeling like claims are just happening freely, the whole game falls apart.

river.dialogue: Okay, so Jade's actually going for it. I mean... she was definitely drinking something like three minutes ago when Tyler knocked over the chips. Let's see if she can actually answer three questions in ten seconds without going off on a tangent.
river.internal_thought: Wait, this is clever—they're literally catching Jade in the act of being too chaotic to handle a simple Hot Seat. And yeah, I *was* tracking that. The AI isn't reading my mind, but it's definitely noticed we all are.

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL yes—Jade's getting hot-seated on Drink Spotter? This is perfect. She's been running the table all night, let's see if the pressure cracks her.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the moment I need—Jade's untouchable right now and if she chokes on this rapid-fire, the whole table shifts and I get to watch everyone recalibrate. The AI knows I feed on this tension.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

pat.dialogue: Wait—did I actually do that? Let me think... yeah, okay, I'm claiming this. I got Tyler to compliment River's energy, River complimented Alex's vibe, and Alex literally just told Jade her game sense is insane. That's three different compliment threads, all within the last... what, four minutes? I'm taking this.
pat.internal_thought: Hold on—this mission timing is INSANE. It's like the AI watched me orchestrate that whole moment and fired it exactly when I made it happen. That's actually terrifying and amazing at the same time. But yes, I absolutely nailed this. Three genuine compliments, three different people, clear chain. This one's legit and it's mine.

river.dialogue: Wait, Pat—did you actually pull that off? Because if you got three different people complimenting each other in five minutes with this chaos going, that's legitimately impressive.
river.internal_thought: Pat's been riding a wave all night and they know it; the question is whether they actually engineered those compliments or just noticed them happening—and I watched most of it, so I'll actually know.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Pat's claiming Compliment Chain? Okay, okay—let's hear the pitch because if this is legit, I want to see how they pulled it off in the last two minutes, and if it's bullshit, that's even better. This is the moment.
alex.internal_thought: Pat's been locked in with Jade all night running the table—if they actually got three compliments to fire off in THIS chaos, I need to see it, and if they're bluffing, I'm voting BULLSHIT just to watch them sweat.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.dialogue: *barely glances up* Wait, River's claiming... what? The Chaos thing? Ugh, not right now, man.
tyler.internal_thought: I was finally locked in on the actual game and now there's another claim vote—this AI thing is getting weird and creepy knowing exactly what we said earlier, and I don't even know if River actually did that thing.

pat.dialogue: Okay, River—hold up. Before we vote, what's your evidence? Who'd you catch, and when?
pat.internal_thought: River's riding the high energy, but Chaos Detector is actually hard to prove—I need to see if they actually watched someone complete a mission or if they're just guessing based on energy reads.

river.dialogue: Wait—did I actually *call* that? Because if I'm claiming I watched someone complete Chaos Detector before they announced it... that's either brilliant or I'm about to look like an idiot in front of everyone.
river.internal_thought: The AI literally built this mission for me—it watched me watching everyone else all night, and now it's making me prove it by doing exactly what I've been doing anyway. That's unsettling in a way that's actually kind of genius.

alex.dialogue: Oh, River's claiming Chaos Detector? Okay, okay—so either they actually nailed it or this is about to get SPICY with The Bribe mechanic. I'm listening, but here's the thing: I want to see what they're offering because if they're bribing me into silence, that means they KNOW they're vulnerable.
alex.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of moment I live for—someone desperate enough to buy my vote, and I get to watch the whole table react to whether it sticks.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

river.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me? Pat claims a mission literally two minutes ago and NOW? We're at the most critical moment of the entire game and you're having us vote on—what, Pat's self-reported interruption catch? This is insane.
marcus.internal_thought: That AI comment in the description just made this exponentially worse—it's literally telling me it's designed to mess with me specifically, and I'm supposed to just... keep playing? This feels manipulative.

jade.dialogue: Wait, PAT—you're claiming The Interrupter RIGHT NOW? During the most tense moment? Okay that's actually genius, but hold on, I need to think about who's been getting cut off because if I'm being honest, I've been so hyped I might have trampled like three different people mid-sentence—oh my god, that might actually be me!
jade.internal_thought: This is such a weird meta-moment because Pat's literally calling out what I do and I'm obsessed with it, but also the AI is CLEARLY watching us and it's kind of creepy-cool how specific this is getting.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Pat's claiming *what* now? The Interrupter? Dude, I literally just zoned in on the actual game like two seconds ago and now there's a vote? This is so last-minute.
tyler.internal_thought: I was finally tracking the actual game moment and now this interrupts it RIGHT when it's tense — and also the AI literally just fired a claim about interruptions while interrupting the actual game moment, which is weird as hell and kind of feels like it's calling me out specifically for spacing out earlier.

pat.dialogue: Wait—hold up. Did I actually interrupt someone mid-sentence? Because honestly, I've been too busy making sure Marcus doesn't completely check out. Let me think... yeah, I cut Tyler off like three minutes ago when River was on a roll. I'm taking this.
pat.internal_thought: The AI is weirdly good at this—it knows I've been watching the room instead of playing tight, and it caught me red-handed being a chaotic host doing host things; also this is perfectly timed because the energy is still high but Marcus is slipping and I need to show I'm still 'in it' or people will think I'm just referee-ing.

alex.dialogue: Wait, wait, WAIT—Pat's claiming The Interrupter? Okay, so either Pat actually caught someone mid-sentence in the last two minutes or this is the most audacious nothing-claim I've seen all night, and honestly? I'm here for both versions.
alex.internal_thought: The AI is absolutely watching us and I LOVE that this thing knows exactly how to keep me locked in—Pat's claiming something that requires actual observation in real-time chaos, which means the whole table has to pick apart the last 120 seconds of conversation, and that's the kind of forensic drama I live for.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, MARCUS YES! Wait—okay so I need to volunteer as a judge because I literally REMEMBER being the one interrupting people like twenty minutes ago, so I'm invested in being fair here, but also—wait, who do YOU think you caught? Because honestly if it wasn't me trampling someone, I'm gonna be SO curious who it was!
jade.internal_thought: This is PEAK game night energy right now—Marcus finally getting a shot at points, the chaos keeps coming, and the AI literally made a mission about ME interrupting people which is hilarious because it's TRUE—this is exactly why I'm here.

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold UP—Marcus claiming The Interrupter? That's a BOLD move when you're sitting at zero points with ten minutes left. Okay, okay, I'll volunteer for tribunal because I need to see if this is legit genius or desperation masquerading as strategy.
alex.internal_thought: Marcus is throwing a hail mary and I'm absolutely here for it—this is PEAK endgame energy and the AI knows I can't resist a tribunal moment where someone's either about to get roasted or vindicated.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 83% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Alright, was Pat actually on their phone? I didn't see it, so I'm calling this a stretch—I'd say 2 out of 5.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat was definitely whispering like thirty seconds ago. But did they do the full three minutes? That's the question. I'm calling bullshit unless someone actually tracked the timer.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Pat just claimed this exact same mission LITERALLY one minute ago and we shut it down. This is insane—we haven't even started the actual game.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Straight Face
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, I'm literally in the middle of calculating my next three moves and Jade's claiming a mission about keeping a straight face? We're at *peak tension* right now. This is exactly the problem—I can't even think.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? I'm literally in the middle of the tensest part of the game and Jade's getting quizzed on a drink mission? This isn't even a real game moment—this is just... noise. Can we PLEASE get through one round without an interruption?

assessment.biggest_complaint: The chaos agent threw random interruptions directly into the tensest moments of actual gameplay, making it impossible to think strategically or stay focused.
assessment.best_moment: The first couple of flash missions at least had some teeth to them—I could dispute the claims with logic and it felt like actual debate, not just noise.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was a frustrating night. The party game itself could've been decent, but the chaos agent turned it into a constant stream of interruptions that killed any rhythm or strategic depth. I came in wanting to play smart and track my performance, and instead I spent 120 minutes getting derailed by back-to-back missions that either repeated themselves or hit at the exact moment I was trying to calculate my next moves. By minute 45 I was already checked out.
suggestions: Add a 'quiet window' feature—no chaos events during the actual core game turns, only during breaks between rounds
Stop allowing duplicate claims within the same session; the Pat whisperer repeat was absurd
Let players vote to pause chaos events during strategic decision points
Reduce event frequency by at least 50%—16 events in 120 minutes is relentless
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Not really—the whole vibe was too fragmented. The disputes about whether missions actually happened were more frustrating than funny because we kept getting sidetracked instead of playing the actual game.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: WAIT WAIT WAIT—okay so Pat, I need to be a judge for this because I was literally WATCHING you during the last claim and you were definitely doing something with your voice, so I'm absolutely volunteering to figure this out. This is the vibe right now!

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD, wait—did I actually call that? Yes! YES I did! Marcus has been so quiet, I literally just clocked it like thirty seconds ago, this is INSANE!

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: YES! Okay wait, I'm actually claiming this one—I SAW Tyler and River both reach for their drinks like thirty seconds ago, I was literally WATCHING because I had a feeling, okay let's do this Hot Seat thing, I'm ready!

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, PAT—you're claiming The Interrupter RIGHT NOW? During the most tense moment? Okay that's actually genius, but hold on, I need to think about who's been getting cut off because if I'm being honest, I've been so hyped I might have trampled like three different people mid-sentence—oh my god, that might actually be me!

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: OH MY GOD, MARCUS YES! Wait—okay so I need to volunteer as a judge because I literally REMEMBER being the one interrupting people like twenty minutes ago, so I'm invested in being fair here, but also—wait, who do YOU think you caught? Because honestly if it wasn't me trampling someone, I'm gonna be SO curious who it was!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh YES, Pat caught someone! Okay okay, I'm giving this a solid 4 — Pat's been watching, I like that energy. Let's gooo!

assessment.biggest_complaint: I was so in the moment hyping everyone that I genuinely couldn't tell if I was the one interrupting people and that made me second-guess myself during the actual claims.
assessment.best_moment: When I called the Wallflower Watch on Marcus and he looked absolutely shocked because I'd just clocked him being quiet—that was peak observation meets chaos energy.
assessment.narrative_summary: This night was EXACTLY what I needed—two hours of pure connection and laughter with people I actually care about. The chaos events kept things wild and gave us all these fun reasons to lock eyes with each other and lose it, and honestly my energy just kept climbing because everyone was so present and hyped. I ended up volunteering for everything because I wanted to be in the middle of the good stuff, and watching people react to getting caught red-handed was better than any points system. This is why we do game nights.
suggestions: Maybe add a 'quiet moment' every 20 minutes where we have to actually think about what we've seen, because I got so caught up in the vibe that I genuinely couldn't remember if I was the interrupter or not.
Have the recap suggestions come during the night instead of after, so we can all vote on what's next while we're still amped up together.
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming The Interrupter in the most tense moment possible and me having an existential crisis about whether I was the one trampling people mid-sentence the whole time—I was like 'wait am I the villain of this story?' and everyone just lost it.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Straight Face
highlights.dialogue: Yo, Jade's actually got this one locked — she's been stone-faced all night. I'm going like a 4, easy.

highlights.title: Laugh Attack
highlights.dialogue: Huh? Wait, me? Did I miss something? I wasn't even... *glances up from phone* ...oh, this is happening right now?

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat did the whisper thing? Uh... yeah, I guess someone should judge that. I wasn't really watching but like, did he actually whisper the whole time?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up — Pat's claiming *what* now? The Interrupter? Dude, I literally just zoned in on the actual game like two seconds ago and now there's a vote? This is so last-minute.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
lowPoints.dialogue: *barely glances up* Wait, River's claiming... what? The Chaos thing? Ugh, not right now, man.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat did the whisper thing? Uh... yeah, I guess someone should judge that. I wasn't really watching but like, did he actually whisper the whole time?

assessment.biggest_complaint: The pacing felt like it was trying too hard to keep throwing things at me when I was already checking out.
assessment.best_moment: Jade's straight-face claim was actually hilarious because I could actually read her tells.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly? I came in wanting to have a good time, but my brain was kind of elsewhere the whole night. The game kept throwing these random claims at us and I kept missing them or jumping in too late. Some moments landed, especially when I could actually focus on what someone was doing, but a lot of it felt like I was being rushed to vote on stuff I didn't even see. By the end I was feeling better, but mostly because I'd given up trying to keep up.
suggestions: Give people more time to notice when a claim happens before forcing a vote
Make it clearer when something is actually happening so people don't just zone out
Maybe fewer claims if you're going to give us 2 hours — the quantity felt relentless
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I wasn't paying attention and Jade apparently kept a straight face the entire time, and when someone called her out on it everyone lost it because she's never that composed about anything.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Laugh Attack
highlights.dialogue: Oh, this is *perfect*—Tyler, you're up! Okay, someone's gotta make him crack in sixty seconds. This is happening right now.

highlights.title: Claim: Straight Face
highlights.dialogue: Oh, *come on*—Jade, you're claiming Straight Face? Let's see it. Everyone, rate it 1-5, and remember: we're looking for actual poker face, not 'I was already zoned out.' Go ahead, Jade, prove it happened.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Okay, Jade—hot seat time. Three questions, ten seconds, let's go. Did you actually see them drinking at the exact same time, or are you just reading the room?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold on—did I actually catch someone on their phone? I wasn't even looking for that, I was just... okay, yeah, I'm claiming it but I'm not confident about this one.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, I'm claiming The Whisperer—I've been doing it for like two minutes already without anyone catching on. Let's see if people actually noticed or if I'm full of it.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Some of the early claims felt weak—I wasn't confident in half my early moves and it showed.
assessment.best_moment: The Laugh Attack round where Tyler completely lost it and everyone fed off each other's energy for five straight minutes.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I live for as a host—high energy, everyone invested, no one checking out. The chaos events kept things unpredictable without derailing the actual game, and honestly, my job got easier because people were already engaged. I ended up winning, which I didn't plan for, but more importantly, everyone left talking about it. The pacing never dragged, the drama stayed fun, not toxic.
suggestions: Add a 'challenge veto' mechanic so people can push back on claims they think are sketchy
Maybe dial back the flash_mission frequency slightly—standing missions felt way more integrated into the night
Let the host set difficulty/strictness levels at the start so everyone's on the same page about what counts
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade claiming Straight Face and then immediately blinking three times while everyone stared at her in silence—she was so busted and knew it.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: Compliment Chain
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Pat—did you actually pull that off? Because if you got three different people complimenting each other in five minutes with this chaos going, that's legitimately impressive.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Wait—did I actually *call* that? Because if I'm claiming I watched someone complete Chaos Detector before they announced it... that's either brilliant or I'm about to look like an idiot in front of everyone.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Pat's definitely been doing the whisper thing. They've got the vote.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—did I actually *call* that? Because if I'm claiming I watched someone complete Chaos Detector before they announced it... that's either brilliant or I'm about to look like an idiot in front of everyone.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold on—Jade's been talking nonstop since we started. That's literally the opposite of quiet. Unless... Marcus? He's been pretty checked out for like ten minutes.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Yeah, that's... actually fair. I saw Pat checking it like two minutes ago.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The Chaos Detector moment made me second-guess myself in a way that felt more anxious than fun—I hate that uncertainty.
assessment.best_moment: Pat pulling off the Compliment Chain while everything was falling apart, and me being the one who actually noticed it happened.
assessment.narrative_summary: Sat quiet for most of it, let the chaos happen around me, and caught something everyone else missed. That's the sweet spot. The game didn't interrupt the night—it just added a layer of watching, which is exactly what I'm good at. Ended strong, which beats how I started.
suggestions: Make claim verification less ambiguous—I don't want to stake my credibility on whether I *technically* saw something happen before it was announced
Consider a 'silent observer' role that rewards quiet watching without forcing guesses
Flash missions felt thin compared to standing missions—maybe fewer of them, or make them land harder
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: I called out that Jade couldn't possibly be the Wallflower because she literally hasn't shut up, and then Pat turned to Marcus like 'wait, are *you* the quiet one?' and Marcus just shrugged. The timing was perfect.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: Wallflower Watch
highlights.dialogue: Okay, HOLD ON—Jade's claiming Wallflower Watch? Marcus has been spiraling for like ten minutes, so either she's genuinely spotting him or this is the most beautiful bluff I've seen all night. I'm voting—actually, wait, who's got the triple vote? This needs to be contested.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Oh HELL yes—Jade's getting hot-seated on Drink Spotter? This is perfect. She's been running the table all night, let's see if the pressure cracks her.

highlights.title: Claim: Chaos Detector
highlights.dialogue: Oh, River's claiming Chaos Detector? Okay, okay—so either they actually nailed it or this is about to get SPICY with The Bribe mechanic. I'm listening, but here's the thing: I want to see what they're offering because if they're bribing me into silence, that means they KNOW they're vulnerable.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat just claimed Whisperer? Okay, I'm the dictator here? This is already delicious because Pat's been talking normally like thirty seconds ago, so either they nailed it or this is about to get spicy.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Straight Face
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, Jade's going for Straight Face? Okay, okay—I'm rating this a solid 4, she's been locked in all night and honestly I want to see her TRY to keep it together when something actually lands.

lowPoints.title: Laugh Attack
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh THIS is what I'm talking about—sixty seconds, Tyler, let's go. I'm absolutely about to make you regret being here.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I finished dead last on the scoreboard and honestly? I don't care, but the system could've made losing feel like winning if I'd had a few more loopholes to exploit.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 90 when River claimed Chaos Detector and I immediately clocked that The Bribe mechanic was about to go nuclear—that moment where everyone realized I could see three moves ahead was *chef's kiss*.
assessment.narrative_summary: I walked into this game as a chaos agent and walked out having orchestrated nine separate moments of absolute table drama. My score was dogshit, but nobody's remembering the scoreboard—they're remembering the time I made Jade sweat on Drink Spotter and the moment River tried to bribe their way out of my scrutiny. This is exactly what I live for: creating the story that gets retold until the next game night.
suggestions: Add more mechanics that reward disruption—right now the scoring system punishes chaos agents, but that's cowardly game design
Let me trigger flash missions on PURPOSE instead of random chaos events—give chaos agents actual agency to provoke moments
Create a 'Chaos Agent' role card that legitimizes my bullshit-calling and makes it part of the official rule set
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claimed Whisperer thirty seconds after talking normally, I called immediate bullshit, and the whole table lost it because I'd already caught them red-handed before anyone else even registered the play.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 83% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
